The final sixty-seven days of Van Gogh's life are examined.

verdict.reason: Essential viewing for Pialat's uncompromising final portrait of artistic madness and the weight of creation. Jacques Dutronc delivers a magnetic performance that captures Van Gogh's fevered energy without romanticizing his suffering.

ratings.plot: Grappling with his past after a life of crime and murder, Robin Hood finds himself gravely injured after a battle he thought would be his last. In the hands of a mysterious woman, he is offered a chance at salvation.

verdict.reason: Wait for word of mouth before committing to Sarnoski's follow-up to Pig. The premise feels thin for two hours and Robin Hood retellings rarely justify their existence.

ratings.plot: In the 1980s, a determined criminal-minded Cuban immigrant becomes the biggest drug smuggler in Miami, and is eventually undone by his own drug addiction.

verdict.reason: De Palma's operatic gangster epic works best for those who can handle three hours of pure excess. The cocaine-fueled madness benefits from theatrical scale, but the runtime demands serious commitment.

ratings.plot: Andy Sachs reunites with Miranda Priestly as they navigate their careers amid the decline of traditional magazine publishing.

verdict.reason: A solid sequel that lacks the original's bite but delivers enough workplace comedy to justify the ticket price. Streep elevates familiar material with a more vulnerable take on Miranda, though the fashion industry satire feels less urgent twenty years later.

ratings.plot: A Manhattan doctor embarks on a bizarre, night-long odyssey after his wife's admission of unfulfilled longing.
A teenage English boy struggles to survive with his fellow captives under Japanese occupation of China during World War II.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing for the airplane sequences and Ballard's Shanghai, but only if you have not experienced it before. Spielberg's spectacle works better theatrically than the coming-of-age story.

ratings.plot: A man and a woman meet by accident on a Sunday evening at their children's boarding school. Slowly the widower and widow reveal themselves to each other, with each revelation hidden by a misperception. They become closer and closer, until she reveals that she can't have a lover because her husband's memory is still far too strong. Much of the film is told wordlessly in actions or through hearing each person's thoughts.
Merlin the magician helps Arthur Pendragon unite the Britons around the Round Table of Camelot, even as dark forces conspire to tear it apart.

Set on the French Riviera in the summer of 1915, Jean Renoir -- son of the Impressionist painter, Pierre-Auguste -- returns home to convalesce after being wounded in World War I. At his side is Andrée, a young woman who rejuvenate...

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're drawn to period pieces about artistic dynasties, but it's more admirable than engaging. Bourdieu brings visual elegance to the Côte d'Azur setting but the pacing never finds its rhythm.

ratings.plot: As an actress begins to adopt the persona of her character in a film, her world becomes nightmarish and surreal.

verdict.reason: Worth the trip if you can commit to three hours of Lynch at his most uncompromising. The digital video texture creates a uniquely disturbing dreamscape that demands big screen immersion.

ratings.plot: After writing a soon-to-be bestselling novel, writer and committed bachelor Harper attempts to hide the fact that his saucy new book is loosely based on the lives and loves of his tight-knit group of friends. Harper is set to be best man at his friend Lance’s wedding, and all his friends will be in attendance. When an advance copy of the book makes its way into the hands of his ex-flame, Jordan, Harper attempts to keep it under wraps.
Anonymous videotapes presage a musician's murder conviction, and a gangster's girlfriend leads a mechanic astray.

verdict.reason: Essential Lynch on the big screen where his sound design and atmospheric dread hit hardest. The film's fragmented narrative structure rewards theatrical attention in ways streaming cannot match.

ratings.plot: A doctor spins an ever-growing web of lies after a taste of the excitement that he was missing in his conventional life, thanks to a chance meeting with a nightclub singer.

verdict.reason: Worth catching if you're already at Film Forum, but this is minor-tier noir that lacks the visual punch of the genre's best. Sherman's direction stays workmanlike when the material needed someone with more bite.

ratings.plot: The problematic lives of teenager students for whom the singer Lily Chou-Chou's dreamy music is the only way to escape an alienating, violent and insensitive society.

verdict.reason: Worth the 146-minute commitment if you connect with Iwai's dreamy blend of teen alienation and ethereal soundscapes. The film works best for viewers willing to absorb its mood rather than follow traditional narrative structure.

ratings.plot: A new teacher at a troubled inner-city high school soon ends up clashing with a delinquent punk posse who run the institute with an iron fist.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you want unapologetic 80s exploitation that takes its violence seriously. Lester directs with genuine menace rather than camp, making this nastier and more effective than most high school revenge fantasies.

ratings.plot: During post-WWII McCarthyism, a diametrically opposed couple come together only to find out that genuine friendship and physical attraction is not enough to overcome fundamental societal beliefs.
New project from Avalon Fast. A story of impossible redemption, modern witchcraft, and duels that repeat themselves like cursed cycles.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're drawn to Fast's singular vision of occult violence, but this feels like homework more than entertainment. The film's recursive structure becomes genuinely hypnotic in the final act, though it demands serious patience to get there.

ratings.plot: The original 1970s TV family is now placed in the 1990s, where they're even more square and out of place than ever.

The aquatic adventure of the highly influential and fearlessly ambitious pioneer, innovator, filmmaker, researcher, and conservationist, Jacques-Yves Cousteau, covers roughly thirty years of an inarguably rich in achievements life.

verdict.reason: Wait for reviews before committing to nearly three hours of Nolan's take on Homer. The IMAX presentation will likely be spectacular but the runtime demands real investment.

movies.title: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu

ratings.plot: Once a lone bounty hunter, Mandalorian Din Djarin and his apprentice Grogu embark on an exciting new Star Wars adventure.

verdict.reason: Only worth seeing if you need your Grogu fix on the biggest screen possible. Favreau directs competently but this feels like an extended TV episode that costs twenty dollars instead of your Disney Plus subscription.

ratings.plot: Newcomers to the country-music business seek love and stardom.
When Casey Connor, Herrington High School's newspaper photographer, witnesses the murder of a nurse and sees her alive again, he decides to investigate the bizarre happenings.
Competition between the maid of honor and a bridesmaid, over who is the bride's best friend, threatens to upend the life of an out-of-work pastry chef.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you want Kristen Wiig at her most unhinged, but the runtime drags in the middle act. Melissa McCarthy steals every scene she's in with perfect physical comedy timing.

ratings.plot: A grieving mother, fueled by her passion for true crime, seeks answers when a teenage girl is found dead in the desert and the police investigation stalls.
A pair of siblings must choose whether to pursue their dream of touring with their rock band or support their family and stay in Cleveland, Ohio.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing for Schrader completists but this is minor-key work from a major filmmaker. Gere and Jett have real chemistry as the sibling musicians, but the family drama never finds the spiritual urgency of Schrader's best films.

ratings.plot: A woman advertising for a new roommate finds that something very strange is going on with the tenant who decides to move in.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you want expertly crafted 90s thriller nastiness that knows exactly what it is. Schroeder directs with the surgical precision he brought to Reversal of Fortune, turning apartment living into genuine claustrophobia.

ratings.plot: Connie is a wife and mother whose 11-year marriage to Edward has lost its sexual spark. When Connie literally runs into handsome book collector Paul, he sweeps her into an all-consuming affair. But Edward soon becomes suspicious and decides to confront the other man.

When her brother decides to ditch for a couple weeks, Viola heads over to his elite boarding school, disguised as him, and proceeds to fall for his school's star soccer player, and soon learns she's not the only one with romantic ...

verdict.reason: Worth a watch if you need a comfort-food gender-swap comedy that actually commits to its Shakespearean source. Amanda Bynes carries the entire film with genuine comic timing that elevates the familiar formula.

ratings.plot: Corrupt Atlantic City police officer Rick Santoro and Navy Commander Kevin Dunn join forces to investigate both the assassination of Secretary of Defense Charles Kirkland and the disappearance of a beautiful stranger.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing for De Palma's trademark long takes and split-screen work, but the third act collapses under its own convoluted plotting. The opening 20-minute sequence shot is a masterclass in camera movement and staging.

ratings.plot: Two terrible lounge singers get booked to play a gig in a Moroccan hotel but somehow become pawns in an international power play between the C.I.A., the Emir of Ishtar, and the rebels trying to overthrow his regime.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you appreciate the ambition behind notorious flops, but the comedy rarely connects despite strong craft. May's direction is more interesting than funny, turning what should be slapstick into something oddly contemplative.

ratings.plot: A famous jazz trumpeter finds himself unable to cope with the problems of everyday life.
Soldiers who were killed in action are brought back to life in a top secret military experiment that creates superhuman warriors.

movies.title: Bumming in Beijing: The Last Dreamers [OV]

ratings.plot: Several stories depicting the landscapes and fauna of India are mixed with documentary footage.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you want Rossellini's outsider perspective on India, but this feels more like commissioned travelogue than essential cinema. The mix of staged drama and documentary footage never coheres into the kind of unified vision that makes his Italian films indispensable.

Worth it if you're curious about Zwartjes' hypnotic Super 8 work from the Dutch underground. The double bill stretches thin at 45 minutes of abstract imagery that rewards patience more than it punishes restlessness.

ratings.plot: Bouchra, a 35-year-old Moroccan coyote in New York documents her long-distance relationship with her mother in Casablanca, as they explore together the love, pain and secrets that unite them through calls and intimate conversations.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing for the fresh take on animated documentary, but the intimate family material works better in a quieter setting than a theater. The filmmakers use animation to bridge geographic distance in ways that feel genuinely innovative rather than gimmicky.

ratings.plot: Police gets a call-out to a lonely hotel in the Alps. When an officer gets to the hotel everything seems to be all right. Suddenly, an avalanche cuts them out from the rest of the world, and strange things are going to happen.

verdict.reason: Essential viewing for anyone interested in Soviet-era genre filmmaking that predates Western sci-fi thrillers by years. Kromanov's atmospheric direction transforms a remote Alpine hotel into a genuinely unsettling psychological maze.

movies.title: My Heart Is That Eternal Rose

ratings.plot: MADDIE’S SECRET is comedian, writer, and actor John Early’s critically acclaimed directorial debut starring himself as Maddie, a plucky dishwasher who leaps to viral superstardom at a trendy food content creation company. While her life seems picturesque – complete with an adoring husband (Eric Rahill), ride-or-die best friend (Kate Berlant), and a cupboard full of woman-owned, ethically-sourced chili crisp to boot – mounting professional pressures threaten to reawaken a hidden secret from her troubled past. A pitch-perfect blend of satire, melodrama, daring tonal shifts, and intimate performances, the film marks a bold new voice in contemporary cinema.
Over two summers, between city streets and forest paths, young art student Adnan has a series of unexpected intimate encounters.

On the last week of school, Alex and Ethan pretend to be trans to get into the girl's locker room. It's all a joke until Ethan realizes she's trans. The two must reckon with their changing friendship and the messy process of comin...

verdict.reason: Worth it for completists of the genre but not essential viewing tonight. The execution is competent without being memorable.

ratings.plot: An intimate documentary following Johnson's journey from surfer to filmmaker to world-renowned musician through rare archives and present-day reflections on how experience, friendship, and exploration shaped his sound and stories.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you want Johnson's music in a theater setting, but the 75-minute runtime suggests this plays more like an extended EPK than essential cinema. Malloy shoots with the reverent distance of a longtime collaborator rather than the probing eye of a documentarian.

movies.title: The Young Film Forum (YFF) Archive Dive: AFTER LIFE
Gregg Allman: The Music of My Soul

ratings.plot: Documentary that chronicles the life and origins of one of the original Allman Brothers Band founders.

verdict.reason: Skip unless you need a straightforward Allman Brothers primer tonight. Director James Keach delivers competent but unremarkable music documentary work that lacks the edge of its subject.

ratings.plot: In the 1800s, after an assassination attempt by Prince Ramon against the king of Mandorra, a brigand resembling the king surreptitiously impersonates the incapacitated monarch in order to throw off the plotters.

verdict.reason: Phil Karlson's swashbuckler has moments of genuine tension but lacks the budget to match its ambitions. The dual role premise gives Anthony Dexter more to work with than his usual leading man parts.

ratings.plot: Exploding out of the AGFA vaults as a brand-new 35mm preservation, this 1990s kung fu fantasia from Hong Kong is a breathtaking combination of action, comedy, and cooking.

The toys are back in Disney and Pixar’s TOY STORY 5, and this time around, it’s Toy meets Tech. Buzz, Woody, Jessie, and the rest of the gang’s jobs get exponentially harder when they go head-to-head with this all-new threat to playtime. It’s been seven years since the last adventure, and the whole crew returns to face a world where imagination competes with something a little more...automated. This new chapter brings the gang back together for a story about loyalty, change, and what happens when play evolves faster than the toys can keep up.

verdict.reason: Skip unless you need the comfort of familiar characters returning after seven years away. The premise of toys versus technology feels recycled from better entries in the series.

ratings.plot: Oscilloscope Laboratories presents CATVIDEOFEST 2026, a compilation of the latest and best cat videos culled from countless hours of unique submissions and sourced animations, music videos, and classic internet powerhouses. Each year, across the country, local theaters partner with nearby cat-focused charities, animal welfare associations, and shelters alike – a portion of ticket proceeds from every show goes directly to local cats in need.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you need a guaranteed crowd-pleaser for a light night out. The communal laughter hits differently in a theater full of cat people than it does on your phone.

movies.title: jackass: best and last Fan Event

ratings.plot: If just hearing the words "Hi, I'm Johnny Knoxville" fires up the pleasure centers of your brain, then JACKASS: BEST AND LAST is the movie event you've been training for your entire life. For 25 years, Knoxville, Steve-O, Wee Man, Chris Pontius, and a rotating cast of willing idiots have dedicated themselves to answering one question: "What if we did something incredibly dangerous, incredibly dumb, and filmed it?" The result is one of the funniest, most painful, and most unexpectedly heartwarming franchises ever created. Now the gang is back for one final ride. JACKASS: BEST AND LAST combines brand-new stunts with the most legendary moments from the series' gloriously reckless history. So gather your buddies, order a drink, and join us for one last victory lap with the patron saints of poor impulse control.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you need the communal experience of watching people hurt themselves with a crowd that gets the joke. The compilation format means you can get the same laughs at home without paying theater prices for recycled material.

movies.title: Gail Daughtry and the Celebrity Sex Pass with Livestream Q&A

ratings.plot: What if your fiancé actually used his celebrity sex pass? That's the question at the center of this gloriously unhinged comedy from WET HOT AMERICAN SUMMER director David Wain. After her devoted high school sweetheart cashes in his hall pass with a famous author, small-town hairdresser Gail Daughtry (Zoey Deutch) heads to Hollywood on a mission to even the score with her own celebrity crush: Jon Hamm (playing himself). Along the way, she'll team up with a psychic, a paparazzo, an overworked assistant, and fellow Mad Men star John Slattery while dodging Italian assassins and chasing the world's most handsome man across Los Angeles. Equal parts romantic comedy, celebrity satire, and increasingly ridiculous road movie, this is the rare film brave enough to ask: what if your relationship problem could be solved by sleeping with Jon Hamm?

Unsuccessfully trying to close old family wounds on a trip back to the Rhode Island home of her miserable childhood, a troubled Brooke Adams finds her new friendship with neighbor Trish Van Devere has her stuck in another family d...

It's a BRAND NEW DAY for Peter Parker. Fighting crime full-time as Spider-Man in a world that doesn’t remember him – and the pressure of seeing his old friends move on without him – sparks a change in Peter he may not have the power to control. But that transformation might also be the only thing that can stop a shocking new threat to the city and those he loves – a powerful villain no one can even see. It’s always a big day when your friendly neighborhood Spider-Man swings into Alamo Drafthouse – and we’re going all in for this one: An exclusive SPIDER-MAN: BRAND NEW DAY menu of drinks and shakes, available at all shows 7/28-8/17. The highest presentation standards in the biz – see it in stunning HDR or on our massive Big Show screens. Our signature ad-free preshow with vintage Spidey clips and other rarities we’ve picked out just for BRAND NEW DAY. The world may have forgotten Peter Parker, but we haven't.

MADDIE’S SECRET is comedian, writer, and actor John Early’s critically acclaimed directorial debut starring himself as Maddie, a plucky dishwasher who leaps to viral superstardom at a trendy food content creation company. While her life seems picturesque – complete with an adoring husband (Eric Rahill), ride-or-die best friend (Kate Berlant), and a cupboard full of woman-owned, ethically-sourced chili crisp to boot – mounting professional pressures threaten to reawaken a hidden secret from her troubled past. A pitch-perfect blend of satire, melodrama, daring tonal shifts, and intimate performances, the film marks a bold new voice in contemporary cinema.

Don’t think ELF is one of the best holiday films ever? You sit on a throne of lies! Christmas movies come and go, but few join the pantheon of the greats. You know the ones – A CHRISTMAS STORY, IT'S A WONDERFUL LIFE, and MIRACLE ON 34TH STREET. These are just a few that are invited to sit at the adult table of holiday movies. While the last couple of decades have been pretty sparse when it comes to quality Christmas movies, ELF, the 2003 Will Ferrell comedy, undoubtedly has taken a corner seat at this proverbial table of fame. Directed by Jon Favreau, ELF is one of those Christmas movies that's impossible to not love It's funny in all the right places, highly quotable, and surprisingly tender. It's Will Ferrell at his Ferrellist. Don't miss a full night of pointy-toed, jingle bell bliss – especially if you need a pick-me-up in the holiday cheer department – because attending ELF is the Christmas spirit equivalent of putting syrup on your spaghetti.

Sam Neill, Laura Dern, Jeff Goldblum, Richard Attenborough, a Tyrannosaurus Rex, and a gaggle of raptors star in director Steven Spielberg's thrilling adaptation of Michael Crichton's novel, boasting still-incredible special effects and action-packed drama. This is Spielberg at his finest, perfectly capturing the awe and horror that would come with seeing dinosaurs return to the land of the living. JURASSIC PARK is one of the most successful films in worldwide box office history, and for good reason. Its action, humor, and heart make it a compelling and spectacular experience. Rediscover this adventure 65 million years in the making, back on the big screen and more beautiful than ever.

movies.title: The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary

ratings.plot: High stakes. Blazing speed. Heart-pounding action. A brotherhood fueled by adrenaline. Welcome to the world of underground street racing. At the turn of the millennium, audiences across the world were introduced to a pulse-pounding, pedal-to-the-metal saga that would forever redefine the action genre... but it started on the streets. Enter THE FAST AND THE FURIOUS, a high-octane thriller that follows undercover cop Brian O'Conner (Paul Walker) as he infiltrates the world of illegal street racing led by charismatic criminal Dominic Toretto (Vin Diesel). As loyalties are tested and relationships forged, an unforgettable partnership is born between the two rivals. Combining the relentless speed of high-performance cars with jaw-dropping action sequences, THE FAST AND THE FURIOUS introduced a new breed of action heroes and sparked a legendary franchise. Witness the birth of a phenomenon that changed the way we experience action movies and ignited our desire for more.

“The most sickly entertaining theatrical experience of the year.” – IndieWire Oh, the things we do for beauty. Look, we’re not saying we wouldn’t want to look like Margaret Qualley, but Demi Moore’s over-the-hill aerobic star goes to some pretty extreme measures. Her use of THE SUBSTANCE may transform her into a “younger, more beautiful, more perfect” version of herself (via a gloriously horrific rebirth), but the more she uses it, the worse the side effects become. It becomes increasingly apparent that beauty is only skin deep as both of her lives spiral into a truly ugly mess. THE SUBSTANCE is a wild, toe-curling ride from writer/director Coralie Fargeat that features some of the most nerve-shredding body horror sequences in recent years. And with it taking home the award for Best Screenplay at the 2024 Cannes Film Festival, there’s plenty of substance behind the camera as well. As fantastical as the beauty solution is, THE SUBSTANCE’s themes of aging, youth, and self-esteem are very much grounded in our obsession with physical beauty.

In this charming little parlor, you don't just get your hair and nails did – you get life lessons from some of the best and sassiest actresses to ever grace the screen. Without STEEL MAGNOLIAS, how would you know that there are so many different shades of pink (including Blush and Bashful), or that all gay men have track lighting? Without Ouiser Boudreaux (Shirley MacLaine, in all of her cantankerously awesome glory), how would you discover that it's possible to be in a bad mood for 40 years? And without Clairee Belcher (Olympia Dukakis), how would you learn that the only thing that separates us from animals is our ability to accessorize? Don't even get us started on Truvy Jones (Dolly Parton), whose folksy wisdom and bedazzled ensembles basically make life worth living. These ladies are guaranteed to inspire you just as sure as they'll make you bawl your eyes out and then crack you up, because we all know laughter through tears is the best emotion. So tease up your hair, grab your bestie, and join us for a cinematic celebration of friendship, perms, and Southern living. It'll be even better than a slice of armadillo cake.

Winner of over 35 awards, experience the best of British musical HER-story in a live capture of the must-see musical sensation, SIX the Musical . The original West End cast reunite at London’s Vaudeville Theatre in front of a sold-out audience to strut their stuff and rewrite their Tudor traumas in an unmissable cinematic recording of the show packed full of style, sass, and sensational songs. Watched by audiences of over 3.5 million, SIX the Musical has become a global theatre phenomenon since its 2017 debut at Edinburgh Festival Fringe, where it has redefined the boundaries of musical theatre. The show tells the extraordinary story of the six wives of King Henry VIII, who step out of the shadow of their infamous husband and reclaim their own narratives.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you love the show enough to sing along in public, but the theatrical energy gets lost in translation to cinema. The original West End cast delivers the vocals with precision, but filmed musicals rarely capture what makes live theater electric.

ratings.plot: Tells the story of the six wives of Henry VIII, reimagining them as pop stars who reclaim their narratives from their infamous husband's shadow.

verdict.reason: Worth catching if you need a Broadway fix without the Broadway prices. The pop concert format works better live than it will on eventual streaming platforms.

movies.title: Talladega Nights: The Ballad of Ricky Bobby 20th Anniversary Re-Release

ratings.plot: Lifelong friends and national idols Ricky Bobby and Cal Naughton Jr. have earned their NASCAR stripes with their uncanny knack of finishing races in the first and second slots, respectively, and slinging catchphrases like "Shake and bake!" But when a rival French driver coasts onto the track to challenge their records, they will have to floor it to retain their top-dog status!

verdict.reason: Skip the anniversary lap unless you need the big screen energy for McKay's NASCAR satire. The theatrical experience adds little that streaming cannot deliver for this comedy.

movies.title: Buffet Infinity + MAN EATING P***Y Double Feature

ratings.plot: Echoing the Canadian comedy classic SCTV and picking from hundreds of hours of original, low-budget TV ads to tell the sinister tale of two restaurants battling it out in the fictional town of Westridge County. Ads for insurance, used car rivals, a local religious scholar, and a recording artist converge to tell the story of an expanding sinkhole, a cult, and an ever-growing restaurant that becomes unsettlingly sentient.

movies.title: Supergirl (2026) HDR by Barco Fan First Screening

ratings.plot: Kara Zor-El, aka Supergirl, has never exactly been in a hurry to team up with anyone, until a ruthless adversary hits way too close to home and forces her into an uneasy alliance with an unlikely companion on a full-throttle, interstellar mission of vengeance and justice. Starring Milly Alcock ( House of the Dragon ) and produced by James Gunn on the heels of 2025’s SUPERMAN, this new chapter sends Supergirl straight into deep space and into trouble with Mad Max-style space pirates who really, really picked the wrong Kryptonian. And yes, she’s not alone: puppy Krypto is along for the ride. PUPPY KRYPTO!

verdict.reason: Skip unless you need to see James Gunn's space pirate take on Supergirl before everyone else. The HDR presentation and early access are the only reasons to commit to this screening over waiting for regular showtimes.

ratings.plot: Kara Zor-El, aka Supergirl, has never exactly been in a hurry to team up with anyone, until a ruthless adversary hits way too close to home and forces her into an uneasy alliance with an unlikely companion on a full-throttle, interstellar mission of vengeance and justice. Starring Milly Alcock ( House of the Dragon ) and produced by James Gunn on the heels of 2025’s SUPERMAN, this new chapter sends Supergirl straight into deep space and into trouble with Mad Max-style space pirates who really, really picked the wrong Kryptonian. And yes, she’s not alone: puppy Krypto is along for the ride. PUPPY KRYPTO!

verdict.reason: Wait for reviews before committing to this James Gunn production that feels more like a setup than a story. The HDR format showcases the space action sequences but the Mad Max meets Superman premise reads thin on paper.

ratings.plot: In MOANA, Disney’s live-action reimagining of the beloved Oscar®-nominated animated adventure, Moana (Catherine Lagaʻaia) answers the Ocean’s call and, for the first time, voyages beyond the reef of her island of Motunui with the infamous demigod Maui (Dwayne Johnson) on an unforgettable journey to restore prosperity to her people.

movies.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day HDR by Barco

Our signature ad-free preshow with vintage Spidey clips and other rarities we’ve picked out just for BRAND NEW DAY. The world may have forgotten Peter Parker, but we haven't.

Shrouded in secrecy! Encircled by intrigue! The Mystery Machine promises to deliver cinematic treasures that span genres, countries, and tastes... only for those brave enough to enter. All shall be revealed only after the credits begin. Screening on select Mondays with discounted admission.

verdict.reason: Only buy a ticket if you enjoy genuine surprises and trust the programmers completely. The mystery format works best when you have zero expectations and ninety-five minutes to spare for whatever unfolds.

ratings.plot: One voice. One unforgettable spectacle. SIA: NOSTALGIC FOR THE PRESENT is a bold, genre-defying experience that trades pop pageantry for pure artistry. Awe-inspiring vocals, breathtaking choreography, and iconic performances, captured cinematically and soundtracked by an unmatched catalog of hits. In 2016, Sia toured the world, mirroring this film live on stage, frame for frame. Remastered for its tenth anniversary, it stands alone for the first time – not as a concert film, but as a visual masterpiece unlike anything in music since. Starring Maddie Ziegler, Kristen Wiig, Paul Dano, Ben Mendelsohn, Gaby Hoffmann, and Tig Notaro. Choreographed by Ryan Heffington. Directed by Sia and Daniel Askill.

First Date is a comedic interactive experience powered by AI, where the audience creates our dating heroes, coaches what they say, and determines whether romantic sparks fly or ensure they crash and burn. Every audience input branches the story in new directions, making each screening a one-night-only experience.

verdict.reason: Skip this unless you enjoy audience participation gimmicks over actual storytelling. The AI-powered interactive format means you're paying theater prices for what amounts to a group video game experience.

movies.title: The Snake Girl and the Silver-Haired Witch

ratings.plot: After years at a Catholic orphanage, a young girl's life takes an unexpected turn when her long-absent father suddenly appears to reclaim her.

verdict.reason: Worth checking out if you're drawn to Japanese B-horror from the late sixties. Yuasa's direction leans heavily on atmospheric Gothic imagery that works better in a theater than at home.

ratings.plot: Imagine that a local cable station was invaded by otherdimensional creatures at the stroke of midnight. That's Video Vortex. Each month, this series presents a mystery transmission that's culled from the outer limits of home video history – from essential underground films to surreal docs, from tokusatsu battle-parties to outsider experiments. So warm up the cathode tubes, dim the lights, and let your senses intermingle with the glow.

verdict.reason: Worth checking out if you're in the mood for genuine surprises from the video store basement. The series delivers legitimate deep cuts that rarely surface elsewhere, though the mystery format means you're buying a ticket blind.

ratings.plot: Imagine that a local cable station was invaded by otherdimensional creatures at the stroke of midnight. That's Video Vortex. Each month, this series presents a mystery transmission that's culled from the outer limits of home video history – from essential underground films to surreal docs, from tokusatsu battle-parties to outsider experiments. So warm up the cathode tubes, dim the lights, and let your senses intermingle with the glow.

verdict.reason: Worth it if you enjoy digging through VHS obscurities with an audience that gets the joke. The curation is solid but the format means you never know if you'll get gold or cheese.

ratings.plot: In the mythical city of Leisure, a sexually-liberated husband and wife seduce a younger, staid couple into experimenting with bisexuality and group sex.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're curious about Metzger's stylish approach to adult cinema, but the theatrical experience adds little to what's essentially an elaborate bedroom farce. The director's trademark visual sophistication elevates the material above typical exploitation fare.

ratings.plot: The live theater capture of the hit musical Hadestown , which won eight Tony Awards in 2019, including Best Musical and the 2020 Grammy Award for Best Musical Theater Album, featuring the five original principals of the Broadway company. The genre-defying new musical blends modern American folk music with New Orleans-inspired jazz to reimagine a sweeping ancient tale. Following two intertwining love stories – that of young dreamers Orpheus and Eurydice, and that of King Hades and his wife Persephone – Hadestown invites audiences on a hell-raising journey to the underworld and back.

movies.title: The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary Movie Party

ratings.plot: High stakes. Blazing speed. Heart-pounding action. A brotherhood fueled by adrenaline. Welcome to the world of underground street racing. At the turn of the millennium, audiences across the world were introduced to a pulse-pounding, pedal-to-the-metal saga that would forever redefine the action genre... but it started on the streets. Enter THE FAST AND THE FURIOUS, a high-octane thriller that follows undercover cop Brian O'Conner (Paul Walker) as he infiltrates the world of illegal street racing led by charismatic criminal Dominic Toretto (Vin Diesel). As loyalties are tested and relationships forged, an unforgettable partnership is born between the two rivals. Combining the relentless speed of high-performance cars with jaw-dropping action sequences, THE FAST AND THE FURIOUS introduced a new breed of action heroes and sparked a legendary franchise. Witness the birth of a phenomenon that changed the way we experience action movies and ignited our desire for more.
Sarah McBride's groundbreaking campaign becomes the first transgender Congressional victory amid anti-trans political tension.

verdict.reason: Chase Joynt brings real filmmaking skill to political biography, but the 93-minute runtime feels rushed for such complex subject matter. The immediacy of McBride's historic win makes this more urgent than most campaign documentaries.

movies.title: I’m the Same, I'm an Other

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're curious about experimental narrative structures, but this isn't essential viewing tonight. The film's commitment to its central gimmick becomes more impressive than engaging as it stretches toward feature length.

ratings.plot: The Biblical story of Salomé, a girl who agrees to perform the "dance of the seven veils" in return for John the Baptist's head on a silver platter.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're drawn to experimental dance cinema, but this feels more suited to a gallery than a theater. The film's static compositions and repetitive structure test patience over its feature length.

ratings.plot: Inspired by Christian mythology, from which it draws a creative transformation force, in a search for identity that questions the two cultures to which the filmmaker belonged.

verdict.reason: Only worth seeing if you're already deep into experimental cinema or have a specific interest in the director's work. The pacing demands patience that most viewers won't have on a weeknight.

ratings.plot: A beautiful, wealthy woman's insatiable appetite for romance leads to tragedy and a police investigation.

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're drawn to intimate character studies, but this won't be essential viewing for most. The film's strength lies in its naturalistic approach to documenting a young woman's emotional journey without forced dramatic beats.

Worth attending if you're interested in Hong Kong video art history, but this is specialized programming. Pau's pioneering work with early digital media deserves the institutional spotlight at this rare retrospective screening.

movies.title: Teo Hernández: The Jaguar Speaks the Language of Flowers

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you want to engage with experimental cinema that demands patience and attention. Hernández's Super 8 work creates hypnotic visual textures that benefit from theatrical projection but will frustrate anyone expecting conventional narrative structure.

movies.title: Teo Hernández: A Journal Is a Measure of Semen in a Die

verdict.reason: Essential viewing for anyone interested in underground queer cinema, but the fragmentary diary structure demands patience with nonlinear storytelling. Hernández's Super 8 work captures raw intimacy in ways that feel genuinely transgressive rather than merely provocative.

movies.title: Teo Hernández: Cluster of Eyes—Michel Nedjar, Jakobois, and MétroBarbèsRochechou Art

verdict.reason: Worth seeing if you're drawn to experimental French cinema from the margins. Hernández's handheld Super 8 work creates an intensely personal visual language that demands patience and rewards attention.

Worth it if you're drawn to experimental documentary work that prioritizes atmosphere over narrative. The film's meditative pacing and abstract structure make it better suited for patient viewers seeking immersive visual poetry.

ratings.plot: A devoted single father adjusts to life after his wife's passing while raising his son with Down Syndrome. They embark on a journey through Metro Atlanta to attend their first baseball game. Throughout their day-long trip, they encounter Murphy's Law. From car breakdowns to missed trains, the duo faces a series of obstacles that test their relationship. Despite the setbacks, they persevere, determined to reach the game.

movies.title: VOICEMAILS FOR ISABELLE & YOU'VE GOT MAIL

ratings.plot:
Voicemails from Isabelle, a new swoon-worthy rom-com written and directed by Leah McKendrick, charts an unexpected romance between Jill (Zoey Deutch, Nouvelle Vague) and Wes (Nick Robinson, Jurassic World). After her sister Isabelle (Ciara Bravo) dies, Jill copes by continuing to leave her voicemails. Wes, an Austin real estate agent, gets a new work phone and is assigned Isabelle’s old number. Unbeknownst to Jill, Wes gets to listen in on her hilariously confessional messages about her life as an aspiring pastry chef in San Francisco. One voicemail at a time, Wes starts to fall in love with Jill, as they sit on different benches across the country from each other. Wes decides to make his feelings known and turns up in San Francisco. But things get even more complicated when he puts off telling Jill the bittersweet reason he’s drawn to her. 



On Saturday, June 20, to celebrate the film’s’ release on Netflix, the Paris Theater is proud to present a special, FREE rom-com double feature of Voicemails from Isabelle and one of the classics of the genre that heavily influenced McKendrick’s vision: 1998’s You’ve Got Mail, written and directed by Nora Ephron and starring Tom Hanks and Meg Ryan! As one character in Voicemails notes, Wes and Jill’s meet cute is “a sick reboot of You’ve Got Mail”, so what better way to experience the film than alongside the classic that inspired it?

verdict.reason: Skip unless you want to experience how a modern rom-com references Ephron's classic in real time. The four-hour runtime makes this more of an endurance test than entertainment.

ratings.plot:
Joseph Mankiewicz's unique intriguing comedy stars Ann Southern, Linda Darnell and Jeanne Crain as three wives who must wait out a long day to learn which of them has lost her husband to another woman.

Just as their boat sets off for the day, Deborah (Crain), Rita (Southern) and Lora Mae (Darnell) receive a letter from the alluring Addie Ross (narrator Celeste Holm) stating she has left town with one of their husbands. Each wife spends the fretful day pondering the state of her marriage and the affection each of their husbands has for Addie. By the end of the day, each woman is convinced she must surely be the betrayed wife.

MARRIAGE STORY is Academy Award nominated filmmaker Noah Baumbach's incisive and compassionate portrait of a marriage breaking up and a family staying together. The film stars Scarlett Johansson and Adam Driver, Laura Dern, Alan Alda, and Ray Liotta co-star.

Troy Maxson makes his living as a sanitation worker in the 1950s Pittsburgh. Maxson once dreamed of becoming a professional baseball player, but was deemed too old when the major leagues began admitting black athletes. Bitter over his missed opportunity, Troy creates further tension in his family when he squashes his son's chance to meet a college football recruiter.

Set in contemporary Iran, A SEPARATION is a compelling drama about the dissolution of a marriage. Simin wants to leave Iran with her husband Nader and daughter Termeh. Simin sues for divorce when Nader refuses to leave behind his Alzheimer-suffering father. Her request having failed, Simin returns to her parents' home, but Termeh decides to stay with Nader.

When Nader hires a young woman to assist with his father in his wife's absence, he hopes that his life will return to a normal state. However, when he discovers that the new maid has been lying to him, he realizes that there is more on the line than just his marriage.

Socially awkward Muriel Heslop wants nothing more than to get married. Unfortunately, due to her oppressive politician father, Muriel has never even been on a date. Ostracized by her more socially adept friends, Muriel runs into fellow outcast Rhonda Epinstalk, and the two move from their small Australian town to the big city of Sydney, where Muriel changes her name and begins the arduous task of redesigning her life to match her fantasies.
Members of a Jazz Band come under suspicion when a beautiful nightclub singer is murdered.

verdict.reason: Worth catching if you need a compact noir fix, but this one lacks the visual style to justify choosing it over better options. At 64 minutes, Hoffman delivers workmanlike mystery plotting without the atmospheric cinematography that makes lesser noirs memorable.

movies.title: The Amazing Circus: The Last Act

ratings.plot: Feature length version of the final two episodes (episode 8 and 9) of The Amazing Digital Circus, showing the conclusion of the cast of characters trapped in a digital realm under the mercy of an unstable ringmaster.

verdict.reason: Only worth the trip if you are already invested in this digital animation series. The feature-length conclusion combines two episodes for theatrical exclusivity, but this feels designed for existing fans rather than newcomers.
